mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth (e.g., >100k jobs/month for 3 months) but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and low inflation environment, as indicated by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), allows the Federal Reserve to maintain an accommodative monetary policy, which supports equity valuations by keeping discount rates low and fostering economic stability.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is between 1.5% and 2.5%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains within the specified range, but S&P 500 60-day returns are flat or negative on average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand fueled by affordable credit. This dual condition boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) during a period of already elevated unemployment signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by > 0.5pp over 3 months while UNRATE is above 6%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer confidence and investment in real estate, indicating a healthy economic environment that typically correlates with positive short-term S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a consistent upward trend (e.g., 3-month average > 1.2M units) but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, reflecting robust economic demand and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO's 3-month annualized growth consistently exceeds 3.0%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative on average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a late-cycle economy where the Federal Reserve is tightening monetary policy to prevent overheating, which often precedes an economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive shift in GDP growth (GDPC1) following a period of contraction signals the beginning of an economic recovery, which typically leads to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets anticipate future earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after at least two consecutive negative quarters
failure_condition: If GDPC1 turns positive after two consecutive negative quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of such recovery periods over a 10-year span.
